reviews.review_description: Make it available for quest 3s

I found this game back in the Oculus Go days and I was so happy to be able to play this game on my Quest Pro. When my mind is going a million directions this game really helps me to unwind and let the outside world go. I love that I can put the controllers down and immerse in the tranquil world and step through the simple puzzles. At the end I feel very relaxed in a similar way to how I feel after meditative experiences.

Great game, beautifully executed, only reason I gave it such a low rating is that it so short it was not good value for money. It took less than 40 minutes to play the whole thing, more like a demo than a complete game

reviews.review_title: Does not allow move to SD
reviews.review_description: Unfortunate that this otherwise great app doesn't allow its storage to be moved to the SD card!

reviews.review_title: The best $5 you will ever spend on VR
reviews.review_description: I didn't know too much about Land's End, other than a short video and good reviews.   Wow, spectacular!  It's extremely unique, spacial, and intriguing all throughout.  Chapter 4 (just finished) is flat-out incredible, visually, with so many unique environments.  Some puzzles take very little thought, others need a bit of experimentation, but the methodology makes it fun.  So happy this crosses over to the Quest.  It is awesome.  Thanks!
For whatever reason my game does not appear on my meta quest VR 3, it did but now it’s gone not to be found or even show at least as downloaded, don’t know what to do…
The creators of Monument did not disappoint with this new adventure. Relaxing sounds, surreal landscape, good puzzles. Very, very enjoyable.
Amazing atmosphere and sound but too short and the puzzles where not challenging enough.

As soon as I saw that this was made by the folks who did Monument Valley, that's all it took for me to purchase.  I was not disappointed! It is an amazing game!

P.s. Is that a Colossus head in the desert?

For a VR game it's great. Came home from work every day got comfy put headphones on and zoned out with this. The sounds are amazing and relaxing while the puzzles are interesting yet not intensely hard to get through. The ending is awesome.
I really enjoyed this game.  It wasn't hard to solve, but it was very enjoyable.  I'll play it again.
The game is very repetitive and you're done within 1 hour. Graphics are very low quality and the sound is constantly the same. Not worth buying.
Low-poly but gorgeous world.  Puzzles are not hard, but still enjoyable. Would love to see more levels.

Just a lovely captivating game! Enjoyable fairly simple "puzzles" to solve, fascinating vistas, no words and cool sound effects,  and all played without the controller. Make sure you are able to swivel easily - a yoga ball works well.

The puzzles were a bit too easy but for the neck movements I can see why. You can use the controller for harder angels but I will definitely sit in a swivel chair the next time. A fantastic job on content and although I could definitely tell  the game graphics were low poly, combined with the ambient occlusion, atmosphere and music it was just beautifully done. Very relaxing, gorgeous immersive fun!
I'm new to vr and this is amazing.
It was kinda made me sick

Despite not featuring a single curve, the blocky graphics feel at home in an alien world I enjoyed venturing around. The gameplay is engaging, if slightly repetitive, and there are some cool reveals. It is short though, you could probably complete it in an hour.

I’m a big fan of the Monument Valley app series, so when I saw this app has the same creator, I had no doubt it would also be an incredible game.
WOW.....is that an understatement. I was BLOWN AWAY with ‘Land’s End’ as the creator now takes you to the next level of ‘Monument Valley’ where you are inside the game itself and reacting to a Virtual Reality World

reviews.review_title: A VR “Close Encounters of the 3rd Kind”
reviews.review_description: Five chapters where you feel like you have superpowers to move rocks and change the course of the universe. The end reminds me of the Steven Spielberg movie but I don’t want to have to include spoiler alerts so just play and you’ll know what I mean. Relax and enjoy an otherworldly experience.
